1	# CampusFit Prior-Issue Recheck (Static) — 2026-04-09
2	
3	## Scope / Method
4	- Static-only recheck against previously reported issues.
5	- No project startup, no Docker, no test execution.
6	- Status values used: Fixed / Partially Fixed / Not Fixed.
7	
8	## Summary
9	- Fixed: 6
10	- Partially Fixed: 1
11	- Not Fixed: 1
12	
13	## Issue-by-Issue Verification
14	
15	### 1) Putaway completion bypass (Blocker)
16	- Previous finding: Receipt could move `PUTAWAY -> COMPLETED` without generated/confirmed putaway tasks.
17	- Current status: **Fixed**
18	- Evidence:
19	  - Backend now blocks completion when no putaway tasks exist: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java:141](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java#L141)
20	  - Backend now auto-generates tasks on transition to `PUTAWAY`: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java:57](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java#L57)
21	  - Regression tests added for both “no tasks generated” fail and auto-generation path: [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:136](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java#L136), [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:149](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java#L149)
22	
23	### 2) Cross-account export/import masking profile identifiers (High)
24	- Previous finding: Export masked profile identity fields and import skipped masked values, risking incomplete migration.
25	- Current status: **Fixed**
26	- Evidence:
27	  - Export now writes raw profile identity fields inside encrypted payload: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:200](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L200)
28	  - Import path still accepts raw values and only skips masked legacy payloads as safety net: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:165](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java#L165)
29	- Note:
30	  - `userId` redaction still exists in exported JSON bundle: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:219](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L219). This does not reopen the previously reported profile-field issue.
31	
32	### 3) Backend test signature mismatch in discrepancy service test (High)
33	- Previous finding: Test method call did not match service signature.
34	- Current status: **Fixed**
35	- Evidence:
36	  - Service signature remains 5-arg: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java:84](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java#L84)
37	  - Test now calls 5-arg method correctly and adds negative paths: [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:156](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java#L156), [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:177](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java#L177)
38	
39	### 4) Frontend-backend status contract drift (High)
40	- Previous finding: FE used statuses not present in BE (`PAUSED` vs `RECALCULATED` / `ARCHIVED`).
41	- Current status: **Fixed**
42	- Evidence:
43	  - Fitness status now aligned in FE with backend (`RECALCULATED`): [repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:49](repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts#L49), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java:96](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java#L96)
44	  - Study status now aligned in FE with backend (`ARCHIVED`): [repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:101](repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts#L101), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java:65](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java#L65)
45	
46	### 5) Sign-out auth contract inconsistency (Medium)
47	- Previous finding: Sign-out behavior/security and spec appeared inconsistent.
48	- Current status: **Fixed**
49	- Evidence:
50	  - Code remains idempotent/public under `/api/auth/**`: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:49](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java#L49), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:47](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java#L47)
51	  - Spec text now explicitly treats sign-out as exception to auth-required endpoints: [docs/api_spec.md:212](docs/api_spec.md#L212)
52	
53	### 6) Missing backend integration-layer tests (Medium)
54	- Previous finding: No `@SpringBootTest`/integration suite to cover DB+security chain interactions.
55	- Current status: **Not Fixed**
56	- Evidence:
57	  - No `@SpringBootTest`, `@DataJpaTest`, or Testcontainers usage found in backend tests during recheck.
58	
59	### 7) “Partition by term and month” only partially evidenced (Medium)
60	- Previous finding: Month partition existed; term-partition evidence was missing.
61	- Current status: **Partially Fixed**
62	- Evidence:
63	  - New term-focused index migration added: [repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V18__add_term_indexes.sql:1](repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V18__add_term_indexes.sql#L1)
64	  - Existing month partitions still present: [repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V16__partition_study_tables.sql:8](repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V16__partition_study_tables.sql#L8)
65	- Remaining gap:
66	  - Still no direct term-based table partitioning DDL shown (indexing is an improvement but not equivalent to partitioning by term).
67	
68	### 8) Unused generic AuditLogService (Low)
69	- Previous finding: Audit service defined but not integrated.
70	- Current status: **Fixed**
71	- Evidence:
72	  - Audit service now injected and used in export/deletion flows: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:58](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L58), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:114](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L114), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:33](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java#L33), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:61](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java#L61)
73	
74	## Final Recheck Verdict
75	- **Most previously reported issues are fixed (6/8), with one partially fixed and one still open.**
76	- Highest remaining priority from prior list: add backend integration-layer tests.
77

1	# CampusFit Static Delivery Acceptance & Architecture Audit (2026-04-09)
2	
3	## 1. Verdict
4	- Overall conclusion: **Partial Pass**
5	- Rationale: The repository is substantial and includes broad module coverage, but there are material defects in a core inbound flow and multiple high-severity consistency/testability risks that block clean acceptance.
6	
7	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
8	- Reviewed:
9	  - Top-level docs and run/test/config instructions ([repo/README.md:35](repo/README.md#L35), [repo/.env.example:15](repo/.env.example#L15), [repo/docker-compose.yml:11](repo/docker-compose.yml#L11), [docs/api_spec.md:212](docs/api_spec.md#L212), [docs/design.md:24](docs/design.md#L24)).
10	  - Backend entry points/security/auth/business modules/migrations/tests under [repo/services/api](repo/services/api).
11	  - Frontend routes/guards/adapters/stores/tests under [repo/apps/web](repo/apps/web).
12	- Not reviewed in depth:
13	  - Every single UI component style/state edge case.
14	  - Third-party library internals.
15	- Intentionally not executed:
16	  - No project startup, no Docker, no tests, no external services.
17	- Manual verification required for:
18	  - Runtime DB migration behavior on real MySQL.
19	  - Real session timeout/lockout timing in live requests.
20	  - End-to-end encryption/decryption interoperability in deployed environment.
21	
22	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
23	- Prompt core goals mapped:
24	  - Offline auth + RBAC + lockout/session handling: implemented in security/auth modules ([repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:49](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java#L49), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthService.java:74](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthService.java#L74), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptService.java:28](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptService.java#L28)).
25	  - Fitness/study/inbound/master-data/notifications/export/reporting modules exist with dedicated controllers/services/entities.
26	  - Encryption-at-rest and export password protection are implemented ([repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Assessment.java:59](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Assessment.java#L59), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:235](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L235)).
27	- Main gaps from prompt:
28	  - Inbound putaway recommendation/confirmation can be bypassed before completion.
29	  - Cross-account export/import handling masks critical profile identity fields and then skips restoring them.
30	  - Static testability has a concrete compile-level inconsistency in backend tests.
31	
32	## 4. Section-by-section Review
33	
34	### 1. Hard Gates
35	
36	#### 1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
37	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
38	- Rationale: Startup/config/test instructions are present and mostly consistent; however, static verifiability is reduced by at least one test-signature mismatch and no integration-level test layer.
39	- Evidence:
40	  - [repo/README.md:35](repo/README.md#L35), [repo/README.md:64](repo/README.md#L64), [repo/.env.example:15](repo/.env.example#L15)
41	  - [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:156](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java#L156)
42	  - [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java:84](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java#L84)
43	  - Web-layer/unit-layer tests only: [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:40](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java#L40), [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:34](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java#L34)
44	- Manual verification note: Runtime behavior cannot be asserted without execution.
45	
46	#### 1.2 Material deviation from Prompt
47	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
48	- Rationale: Implementation is largely centered on prompt scope, but core inbound putaway semantics are weakened by bypass paths.
49	- Evidence:
50	  - Prompt-aligned modules exist: [repo/services/api/README.md:13](repo/services/api/README.md#L13)
51	  - Bypass risk: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java:113](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java#L113), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:117](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java#L117)
52	
53	### 2. Delivery Completeness
54	
55	#### 2.1 Core explicit requirements coverage
56	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
57	- Rationale: Most core flows are present, but there are high-impact mismatches in inbound completion logic and cross-account export/import identity handling.
58	- Evidence:
59	  - Fitness recalculation + audit: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicy.java:30](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicy.java#L30)
60	  - Inbound state machine: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java:56](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java#L56)
61	  - Export/import masking behavior: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:191](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L191), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:165](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java#L165)
62	
63	#### 2.2 End-to-end deliverable vs demo fragment
64	- Conclusion: **Pass**
65	- Rationale: Full multi-module structure with backend/frontend/docs/migrations/tests exists; this is not a single-file sample.
66	- Evidence:
67	  - Repository structure and module depth: [repo/README.md:1](repo/README.md#L1)
68	  - Multiple backend modules and migrations: [repo/services/api/README.md:13](repo/services/api/README.md#L13), [repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__create_auth_tables.sql:1](repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__create_auth_tables.sql#L1)
69	
70	### 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality
71	
72	#### 3.1 Structure and decomposition
73	- Conclusion: **Pass**
74	- Rationale: Domain modules are clearly decomposed with controller/service/repository/entity layers.
75	- Evidence:
76	  - Architecture decomposition: [docs/design.md:107](docs/design.md#L107)
77	  - Package organization reflected in code tree under [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit)
78	
79	#### 3.2 Maintainability and extensibility
80	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
81	- Rationale: Structure is extensible, but there are signs of drift in shared contracts (status enums/types) and an unused generic audit service.
82	- Evidence:
83	  - Front/backend status mismatch: [repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:49](repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts#L49), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java:96](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java#L96)
84	  - Study status mismatch: [repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:101](repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts#L101), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java:65](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java#L65)
85	  - Unused audit service: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/audit/AuditLogService.java:8](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/audit/AuditLogService.java#L8)
86	
87	### 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism
88	
89	#### 4.1 Error handling/logging/validation/API design
90	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
91	- Rationale: Validation and global exception handling are present; logging exists and masks some values. However, test inconsistency and contract drift reduce professional reliability.
92	- Evidence:
93	  - Global error handling: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java:17](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java#L17)
94	  - Logging levels: [repo/services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml:60](repo/services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml#L60)
95	  - Masking used in export logs: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:99](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L99)
96	
97	#### 4.2 Product-grade vs demo
98	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
99	- Rationale: Delivery is product-shaped, but core workflow bypass and high-impact data migration inconsistency are not demo-level minor issues.
100	- Evidence:
101	  - Product-level modules and routes: [repo/apps/web/README.md:20](repo/apps/web/README.md#L20), [repo/services/api/README.md:13](repo/services/api/README.md#L13)
102	  - Core workflow bypass evidence cited in Issues section.
103	
104	### 5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
105	
106	#### 5.1 Business-goal and constraint fit
107	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
108	- Rationale: The implementation generally understands the business scope; key constraints are partially weakened: putaway recommendation acceptance, cross-account migration semantics, and some contract consistency points.
109	- Evidence:
110	  - Prompt-fit modules: [docs/design.md:11](docs/design.md#L11)
111	  - Putaway bypass: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java:113](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java#L113)
112	  - Cross-account export conflict with internal assumption: [docs/question.md:207](docs/question.md#L207), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:191](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L191)
113	
114	### 6. Aesthetics (frontend-only/full-stack)
115	
116	#### 6.1 Visual/interaction quality
117	- Conclusion: **Cannot Confirm Statistically**
118	- Rationale: UI files and tests exist, but visual quality/interaction smoothness requires runtime rendering and manual interaction.
119	- Evidence:
120	  - UI route/page coverage: [repo/apps/web/src/app/router.ts:1](repo/apps/web/src/app/router.ts#L1)
121	  - UI test presence: [repo/apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts](repo/apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts)
122	- Manual verification note: Run manual browser QA for layout/spacing/feedback consistency.
123	
124	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
125	
126	### Blocker
127	
128	1. Severity: **Blocker**
129	- Title: Putaway completion can bypass putaway task generation/acceptance
130	- Conclusion: **Fail**
131	- Evidence:
132	  - Completion checks only pending tasks (empty list passes): [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java:113](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachine.java#L113)
133	  - Task generation is optional API branch: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:117](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java#L117), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:122](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java#L122)
134	  - Frontend uses only task-fetch and task-complete paths; no generation call path before completion: [repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:117](repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts#L117), [repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:14](repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts#L14)
135	- Impact: A receipt can reach `COMPLETED` without confirmed putaway recommendations, violating a critical inbound business constraint.
136	- Minimum actionable fix:
137	  - Enforce `PUTAWAY -> COMPLETED` only if putaway tasks exist and all are completed.
138	  - Auto-generate putaway tasks on transition to `PUTAWAY` or require explicit generation step.
139	  - Add backend test to assert completion is blocked when no putaway tasks exist.
140	
141	### High
142	
143	2. Severity: **High**
144	- Title: Cross-account export/import masks and skips restoring profile identifiers
145	- Conclusion: **Fail**
146	- Evidence:
147	  - Export masks username/email/phone: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:191](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L191)
148	  - Export rewrites userId values: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:210](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java#L210)
149	  - Import skips masked profile fields: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:165](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java#L165), [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:175](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java#L175)
150	  - Internal requirement note says raw profile in encrypted export is needed for migration: [docs/question.md:207](docs/question.md#L207)
151	- Impact: “Cross-account migration” can silently lose identity/profile metadata, causing incomplete migration outcomes.
152	- Minimum actionable fix:
153	  - Define a clear migration payload contract: keep sensitive profile fields unmasked inside password-encrypted export payload, while masking only logs/responses.
154	  - Add import validation/warnings for masked or incomplete payloads.
155	
156	3. Severity: **High**
157	- Title: Backend test suite contains a static signature mismatch
158	- Conclusion: **Fail**
159	- Evidence:
160	  - Test calls 4-arg resolve: [repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:156](repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java#L156)
161	  - Service requires 5 args: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java:84](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java#L84)
162	- Impact: Static verifiability and confidence in discrepancy logic coverage are reduced; CI/test pass cannot be trusted until this mismatch is corrected.
163	- Minimum actionable fix:
164	  - Update test invocation to match service signature and include `receiptId` + `resolvedBy`.
165	  - Add a negative test for receipt/discrepancy mismatch path.
166	
167	4. Severity: **High**
168	- Title: Frontend-backend status contract drift in core domain models
169	- Conclusion: **Fail**
170	- Evidence:
171	  - Frontend fitness status includes `PAUSED`: [repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:49](repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts#L49)
172	  - Backend fitness status uses `RECALCULATED` instead: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java:96](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java#L96)
173	  - Frontend study status includes `PAUSED`: [repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:101](repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts#L101)
174	  - Backend study status uses `ARCHIVED`: [repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java:65](repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java#L65)
175	- Impact: UI logic/state rendering can break or silently mis-handle statuses; long-term maintainability degrades.
176	- Minimum actionable fix:
177	  - Establish one shared status contract and align FE/BE enums plus mapping tests.
178	
179	### Medium
180	
181	5. Severity: **Medium**
182	- Title: API auth contract inconsistency for sign-out endpoint

-- V19: Add term-based RANGE partitioning to t_study_plan
-- =====================================================
-- Context: V16 covered month-based partitioning on t_daily_completion and
-- t_forgetting_point.  V18 added composite term indexes.  This migration
-- completes the "partition by term and month" performance commitment by
-- adding explicit RANGE partitioning to t_study_plan on term_id so that
-- queries scoped to a term use partition pruning instead of a full table scan.
--
-- MySQL constraint: Partitioned InnoDB tables do not support foreign keys
-- (neither on the partitioned table itself nor from other tables that reference
-- it).  All FK constraints involving t_study_plan are dropped here; referential
-- integrity for these relationships is enforced at the service layer.

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 1: Drop FKs on child tables that reference t_study_plan
--         (MySQL disallows other tables from using FK references to a
--          partitioned table's primary key)
-- -------------------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE t_study_plan_item   DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_study_item_plan;
ALTER TABLE t_daily_completion  DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_daily_comp_plan;
ALTER TABLE t_forgetting_point  DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_forget_plan;
ALTER TABLE t_streak            DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_streak_plan;

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 2: Drop the outgoing FK on t_study_plan itself
--         (a partitioned table may not carry FK constraints)
-- -------------------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE t_study_plan DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_study_plan_user;

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 3: Make term_id NOT NULL
--         The partition column cannot be nullable; rows with no assigned term
--         receive term_id = 0 and land in the p_no_term partition.
-- -------------------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE t_study_plan
    MODIFY COLUMN term_id BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 4: Rebuild the primary key as a composite (id, term_id)
--         MySQL requires every unique index — including the PK — to include
--         all partition columns.
-- -------------------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE t_study_plan
    DROP PRIMARY KEY,
    ADD PRIMARY KEY (id, term_id);

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 5: Apply RANGE partitioning on term_id
--         Boundaries use sequential ID buckets that map to academic-term IDs
--         seeded by master-data imports.  New buckets are added via
--         ALTER TABLE t_study_plan ADD PARTITION as additional terms are created.
-- -------------------------------------------------------
ALTER TABLE t_study_plan
    PARTITION BY RANGE (term_id) (
        PARTITION p_no_term     VALUES LESS THAN (1),    -- term_id = 0  (no term assigned)
        PARTITION p_term_1_10   VALUES LESS THAN (11),   -- terms  1 – 10
        PARTITION p_term_11_20  VALUES LESS THAN (21),   -- terms 11 – 20
        PARTITION p_term_21_30  VALUES LESS THAN (31),   -- terms 21 – 30
        PARTITION p_term_31_50  VALUES LESS THAN (51),   -- terms 31 – 50
        PARTITION p_term_51_100 VALUES LESS THAN (101),  -- terms 51 – 100
        PARTITION p_future      VALUES LESS THAN MAXVALUE
    );
"}

1	-- =====================================================
2	-- V19: Add term-based RANGE partitioning to t_study_plan
3	-- =====================================================
4	-- Context: V16 covered month-based partitioning on t_daily_completion and
5	-- t_forgetting_point.  V18 added composite term indexes.  This migration
6	-- completes the "partition by term and month" performance commitment by
7	-- adding explicit RANGE partitioning to t_study_plan on term_id so that
8	-- queries scoped to a term use partition pruning instead of a full table scan.
9	--
10	-- MySQL constraint: Partitioned InnoDB tables do not support foreign keys
11	-- (neither on the partitioned table itself nor from other tables that reference
12	-- it).  All FK constraints involving t_study_plan are dropped here; referential
13	-- integrity for these relationships is enforced at the service layer.
14	
15	-- -------------------------------------------------------
16	-- Step 1: Drop FKs on child tables that reference t_study_plan
17	--         (MySQL disallows other tables from using FK references to a
18	--          partitioned table's primary key)
19	-- -------------------------------------------------------
20	ALTER TABLE t_study_plan_item   DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_study_item_plan;
21	ALTER TABLE t_daily_completion  DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_daily_comp_plan;
22	ALTER TABLE t_forgetting_point  DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_forget_plan;
23	ALTER TABLE t_streak            DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_streak_plan;
24	
25	-- -------------------------------------------------------
26	-- Step 2: Drop the outgoing FK on t_study_plan itself
27	--         (a partitioned table may not carry FK constraints)
28	-- -------------------------------------------------------
29	ALTER TABLE t_study_plan DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_study_plan_user;
30	
31	-- -------------------------------------------------------
32	-- Step 3: Make term_id NOT NULL
33	--         The partition column cannot be nullable; rows with no assigned term
34	--         receive term_id = 0 and land in the p_no_term partition.
35	-- -------------------------------------------------------
36	ALTER TABLE t_study_plan
37	    MODIFY COLUMN term_id BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
38	
39	-- -------------------------------------------------------
40	-- Step 4: Rebuild the primary key as a composite (id, term_id)
41	--         MySQL requires every unique index — including the PK — to include
42	--         all partition columns.
43	-- -------------------------------------------------------
44	ALTER TABLE t_study_plan
45	    DROP PRIMARY KEY,
46	    ADD PRIMARY KEY (id, term_id);
47	
48	-- -------------------------------------------------------
49	-- Step 5: Apply RANGE partitioning on term_id
50	--         Boundaries use sequential ID buckets that map to academic-term IDs
51	--         seeded by master-data imports.  New buckets are added via
52	--         ALTER TABLE t_study_plan ADD PARTITION as additional terms are created.
53	-- -------------------------------------------------------
54	ALTER TABLE t_study_plan
55	    PARTITION BY RANGE (term_id) (
56	        PARTITION p_no_term     VALUES LESS THAN (1),    -- term_id = 0  (no term assigned)
57	        PARTITION p_term_1_10   VALUES LESS THAN (11),   -- terms  1 – 10
58	        PARTITION p_term_11_20  VALUES LESS THAN (21),   -- terms 11 – 20
59	        PARTITION p_term_21_30  VALUES LESS THAN (31),   -- terms 21 – 30
60	        PARTITION p_term_31_50  VALUES LESS THAN (51),   -- terms 31 – 50
61	        PARTITION p_term_51_100 VALUES LESS THAN (101),  -- terms 51 – 100
62	        PARTITION p_future      VALUES LESS THAN MAXVALUE
